title: What part of me doesn't actually want this?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for mapping hidden resistance to wealth.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 20 of the MONEY program, using the Week 3 prompts from the MONEY Journal. Use when self-sabotage is running on a pattern you haven't yet been able to name.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 20 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 3 daily prompts of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

content.text: A journal prompt set from Day 20 of the MONEY program. These prompts ask a question most money-work avoids: not "why can't you have it" but "is there a part of you that doesn't want it?" The answer is often more useful than it is comfortable. Twenty minutes, no editing.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
If a large sum of money landed in my account today, what doubts or fears might come up?
Not what you think should happen. What you know from past experience would actually happen in your body and mind.
What do those fears reveal about my readiness to receive?
Write plainly. No judgment on the answer.
What part of me doesn't actually want this?
Write from that part's point of view. What is it protecting you from? What does it think will happen if you have it?
What has that part of me been trying to keep safe?
Name the specific fear underneath the resistance. The more specific the better.
If that protection were no longer needed, what would open up?
Write what you'd do differently, how you'd move, what you'd stop second-guessing.
Adapted from Day 20 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 3 daily prompts of The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
